records.specific_details: Advocating for fair, proportionate and accessible funding for the public service content on independent radio stations in the Budget and in policy discussions. Seeking level playing field.

Fair share of Sound and Vision funding to be allocated to independent radio.::Measures to ensure a level playing field in regulatory and legislative terms for independent stations and fair application of State Aid in the media landscape, as well as unrestricted and prominent access for listeners to radio in vehicles or on devices or platforms. ::Budget allocation for accessible funding for investment in capital and maintenance of emergency communications transmission equipment.::Attendance invited for event aimed at highlighting need to protect prominent access for radio, and arrangement of meetings and highlighting IBI policy priorities

records.specific_details: Mercosur Trade Deal
records.intended_results: That the Government and MEPs must honour their commitments to stand up for farmers and defend their interests by opposing the current Mercosur trade deal.::To discuss farmers' concerns with the Minister for Foreign Affairs::To reiterate strong opposition to the Mercosur agreement in its current form. The beef industry in Ireland supports 90,000 farmers and around 25,000 jobs and is critical to the rural economy. There are very strong concerns about the impact the EU-Mercosur trade agreement would have on the viability of some 90,000 families.::That, while it is often argued that an additional 99,000t of beef from the Mercosur block countries would represent only 1-2% of the EU beef market, this statistic is in fact very misleading. The high value EU steak market (fillet, striploin, and cube) will be targeted by Mercosur beef exporters and will impact EU producers' ability to achieve a positive return on the carcass. 30% of the value of the carcass is contained within the yield of high

records.specific_details: Seeking Government measures to support the continued recovery of Ireland's tourism and hospitality industry
records.intended_results: Restoration of the 9% rate of VAT for hospitality food services::Measures to improve cost competitiveness and avoid any further cost increases::For a review to be carried out of options for assisting hotels to develop employer-led staff accommodation across the sector::Targeted funding for a national hotel retrofitting scheme to reduce carbon footprint::Increased investment and support for tourism marketing both domestically and overseas.::Insurance reform and policy to avoid any additional increases in insurance costs::Increased investment in training and skills development within the sector::Robust enforcement of regulations around short term lettings::Additional resources for tourism economic research::Avoid the introduction of a tourist tax::Increased air access including removal of cap at Dublin airport

records.specific_details: Bovine TB - Spring Lobbying 2025
records.intended_results: That the Minister must meet with IFA to agree on and implement a consistent and adequately resourced TB scheme, including dealing with infected wildlife, in all counties.::To highlight that this is an issue that requires urgent and meaningful actions to be brought back under control, but these actions must be well thought out, have a robust scientific basis and quantifiable in the contribution they will make to reducing TB levels.::That the measures and the entirety of the programme must have transparent KPI's that allow real time monitoring of effectiveness in reducing TB levels and where not contributing to these targets there must be flexibility within the programme to have them removed. All measures in the programme must refer to and identify the scientific studies that informed their inclusion in the programme.::That,  while there is an urgency to enhance the programme, it should not be at the expense of the time necessary to identify the most appropriate and effective measures th

records.specific_details: Nitrates Derogation - IFA Spring Lobbying 2025
records.intended_results: That the Government must redouble its efforts to lobby for the retention of the Derogation, honour its commitments on nutrient storage, and continue to focus on improving water quality from all sources.::To seek clarification on several critical matters. During a meeting with the Minister for Agriculture, Food, & the Marine, IFA were informed that the European Commission has requested the Irish Government to demonstrate more detailed compliance with the Habitats Directive as part of Ireland's application for the renewal of the nitrates derogation beyond 2025::To request that, in the interest of transparency, it is vital that the formal written communication from the Commission outlining this request is made available to stakeholders as a matter of urgency.::To demonstrate serious concerns that the requirement to prepare an Appropriate Assessment (AA) plan at catchment and/or sub-catchment level introduces significant additional legal and administrative burdens on Irish farmers. records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Lobbying regarding Cork-based services.
records.intended_results: Increased awareness, action on implementing services for people with Parkinson's and increased government spending on this area.
lobbying_activities: Email

records.specific_details: Briefings providing to Members of the Joint Committee on Media, and Members of the Oireachtas legislation. Meetings with Minister for Minister for Culture, Communications and Sport, and officials, re Budget/Programme for Government funding. Engagement with the Gambling Regulation Authority of Ireland and Coimisiún na Meán and Comreg on mutual priorities. Communications with MEPs and EU Commissioner on EU legislation.
records.intended_results: To have the priorities, concerns and proposals of our organisation's Members considered in hearings, deliberations and report of the Joint Committee and for changes where necessary to be considered.::To urgently progress the access of our sector to funding approved in the last Budget and in the Programme for Government for news and current affairs in independent radio sector.::To seek to have the priorities and concerns of commercial broadcasters in Ireland and Europe considered in relation to EU and Irish legislation on media.::To ensure that IBI was aware of and understood the plans and schedule for the introduction of gambling advertising regulation.::To ensure open and constructive communications with regulatory authorities including strategic plans for the radio sector.::To arrange meetings or ask questions re policy, regulatory or legislative issues.

lobbying_activities: Access to fair and proportionate funding for independent broadcasters, introduction of new services such as DAB. - Letter

Access to fair and proportionate funding for independent radio sector, general updates on industry and regulatory issues; provision of information by IBI on the vital emergency communications services carried out by Members and the need for support. - Virtual Meeting
Leinster House and nearby.  Meetings to explain concerns and priorities of IBI re funding and legislation. - Meeting
Provision of accessible funding, arranging and seeking meetings, and providing information on IBI's priorities and proposals and arranging or seeking meetings. - Email
Regulatory strategy for media sector and methods of combating misinformation and disinformation. - Event / Reception
Seeking meetings or providing links to content. - Social Media
Upcoming and current legislation and delivery of accessible funding in line with Programme for Government. - Phone call

records.specific_details: Launch of Samaritans Ireland Impact Report and caller research
records.intended_results: Prioritise a National Loneliness Strategy within the Taoiseach's office and establish national measurement standards to effectively address its impact. Mandate Very Large Online Platforms to ensure content moderator wellbeing through transparent reporting, comprehensive mental health support standards, and full inclusion under the Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act. Commit to Samaritans training for all party staff to ensure responsible language on self-harm and suicide
